$schemaNote: Brief 149 — hand-override overlay. The maintainer's per-book decisions, re-asserted as a deterministic TAIL AFTER the auto apply/rebuild by scripts/apply-curation-overlay.ts (a tail because apply-override.ts rebuilds the work_* junctions per-workId delete-then-insert, so a hand fix must run last). ONLY `final` is applied; `reviewQueue` is carried machine-readably (proposals from the B11 book-reviewer / weekly-refresh) and is never applied until promoted to `final`. Edges are scoped to the exact (workId, entityId): add=upsert, remove=delete. Provenance (sourceKind/confidence/checkedAt/note) lives here, not in the junction rows — no DB column, no migration (narrator model). No facet section by design: the overlay structurally cannot write work_facets, so it can never reintroduce a content_warning into the visitor UI (Brief 149/150). Doc: scripts/seed-data/curation-overlay.README.md. NOTE: this example is COMMITTED but, under the Brief-149 DB-freeze, was NOT applied to prod.

add.note: Eisenhorn operates under Malleus authority in the title novella; the SSOT/auto pass only tagged Ordo Xenos.

remove.note: The auto pass tagged Chaos only as faint background; the book's actual antagonists are the Necrons + a Mechanicus heresy. Trim the generic Chaos chip so the faction filter is honest.

format.note: The Magos is structurally a collection of 10 reprinted Eisenhorn shorts anchored by one new novella; the SSOT roster classifies it as 'novel'. This matches the data_conflict already noted in manual-overrides-ssot-w40k-001.json (W40K-0010).

add.note: PROPOSAL ONLY (not applied). Example of a B11-reviewer suggestion awaiting maintainer promotion to `final`. The referenced id may not exist yet — reviewQueue is intentionally NOT reference-checked.
